Title: Verify that actual deviation is detected when ETA to the next stop increases by more than the configured threshold X minutes
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Driver is en route to the next stop - ETA deviation threshold X is configured in system settings (e.g. 5 minutes) - GPS tracking is active
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip - 3- Simulate a scenario where the driver's ETA to the next stop increases by more than X minutes (e.g. X+2 minutes) - 4- Observe whether a deviation alert or indicator is triggered
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Active trip is visible on the map - 3- ETA increase of greater than X minutes is registered by the system - 4- System detects and flags the deviation - a deviation indicator or alert is displayed for the affected trip stop

Title: Verify that no deviation alert is triggered when ETA to next stop increases by less than the configured threshold X minutes
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - ETA deviation threshold X is configured (e.g. 5 minutes) - Driver's ETA increases by X-1 minutes
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip - 3- Simulate an ETA increase that is less than the configured threshold (e.g. threshold is 5 minutes - ETA increases by 4 minutes) - 4- Observe whether any deviation alert is triggered
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Active trip is visible - 3- ETA increases by less than threshold value - 4- No deviation alert or indicator is triggered - system continues normal monitoring without flagging the trip

Title: Verify that no deviation alert is triggered when ETA increase equals exactly the configured threshold X minutes (boundary condition)
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - ETA deviation threshold X is configured - Driver's ETA increases by exactly X minutes
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip - 3- Simulate an ETA increase equal to exactly the configured threshold (e.g. ETA increases by exactly 5 minutes when X=5) - 4- Observe whether a deviation alert is triggered
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Active trip is visible - 3- ETA increases by exactly X minutes - 4- System behavior is consistent with the configured rule (either alert triggered or not based on whether rule is strictly greater-than or greater-than-or-equal) - behavior matches the specification

Title: Verify that the ETA deviation threshold X is system configurable via system settings
Preconditions: User is logged in as System Administrator - System configuration panel is accessible - Current ETA deviation threshold is set to a known value
Steps(Step): 1- Log in as System Administrator - 2- Navigate to the system configuration or settings panel - 3- Locate the ETA deviation threshold parameter - 4- Change the threshold value from the current value to a new value (e.g. from 5 to 10 minutes) - 5- Save the configuration - 6- Trigger an ETA increase on an active trip that falls between the old and new threshold values - 7- Verify the system applies the new threshold
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Admin portal loads - 2- System configuration panel is accessible - 3- ETA deviation threshold parameter is visible and editable - 4- Threshold value is changed successfully - 5- Configuration is saved without errors - 6- ETA increase between old and new threshold does not trigger alert after configuration change - 7- System applies the newly configured threshold for deviation detection

Title: Verify that the updated ETA deviation threshold X takes immediate effect for subsequent deviation checks after configuration change
Preconditions: User is logged in as System Administrator - ETA deviation threshold has just been updated and saved - An active trip is in progress
Steps(Step): 1- Log in as System Administrator - 2- Update the ETA deviation threshold to a new value (e.g. from 5 to 3 minutes) - 3- Save the configuration - 4- Without restarting the application simulate an ETA increase of 4 minutes on an active trip - 5- Observe whether the deviation is detected using the new threshold
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Admin portal loads - 2- Threshold is updated - 3- Configuration saved - 4- ETA increases by 4 minutes (above new threshold of 3 but below old threshold of 5) - 5- System detects deviation using the new threshold and triggers an alert - no restart required

Title: Verify that deviation is NOT detected when ETA increase occurs during driver travel to the start of a break
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Driver is currently traveling to the start of a scheduled break location - ETA for the break start increases by more than X minutes
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip where driver is traveling to break start - 3- Simulate an ETA increase of more than X minutes for the break start location - 4- Observe whether a deviation alert is triggered
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Active trip with break travel segment is displayed - 3- ETA to break start increases by more than X minutes - 4- System does NOT trigger a deviation alert - break travel segments are excluded from deviation detection logic

Title: Verify that deviation detection resumes normally for the next passenger stop after a break segment is completed
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - Driver has completed a break and is now traveling to the next passenger stop - ETA deviation threshold is configured
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip - 3- Confirm driver has completed break and is now en route to next passenger stop - 4- Simulate an ETA increase of more than X minutes for the next passenger stop - 5- Observe whether deviation is detected
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Active trip is displayed - 3- Driver is confirmed past the break segment - 4- ETA to next passenger stop increases by more than X minutes - 5- System detects and flags the deviation correctly - deviation detection is active again after break

Title: Verify that deviation is NOT detected when ETA increase occurs during driver travel to the pull-in location at end of shift
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - Driver is traveling to the pull-in (depot or garage) location at the end of their shift - ETA for pull-in increases by more than X minutes
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for a trip where driver is heading to pull-in at end of shift - 3- Simulate an ETA increase of more than X minutes for the pull-in location - 4- Observe whether a deviation alert is triggered
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Driver end-of-shift pull-in travel is visible - 3- ETA to pull-in location increases by more than X minutes - 4- System does NOT trigger a deviation alert - pull-in travel is excluded from deviation detection logic

Title: Verify that deviation detection correctly identifies the next stop ETA (not a subsequent or previous stop) for comparison
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip with multiple upcoming stops is in progress - ETA values exist for all future stops - Driver is approaching Stop 2 with Stops 3 and 4 ahead
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the trip detail for an active trip with multiple upcoming stops - 3- Note ETA values for the next stop (Stop 2) and subsequent stops - 4- Simulate an ETA increase only for the next stop (Stop 2) - 5- Observe which stop's ETA is used for deviation detection
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Trip detail is displayed with multiple upcoming stops - 3- ETA values for all stops are noted - 4- ETA for next stop (Stop 2) increases by more than X minutes - 5- Deviation is detected and flagged against the next stop (Stop 2) only - subsequent stops are not the basis for this detection

Title: Verify that deviation alert clears or updates when ETA for the next stop reduces back below the threshold after being flagged
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip has a deviation alert triggered (ETA exceeded threshold) - Traffic conditions improve and ETA reduces back below threshold
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip with deviation alert active - 3- Simulate traffic improvement causing ETA to drop below the deviation threshold - 4- Observe whether the deviation alert clears or updates
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Deviation alert is active for the trip - 3- ETA reduces below threshold - 4- System updates and removes or clears the deviation alert to reflect the improved ETA - no stale alerts remain

Title: Verify that deviation detection handles multiple consecutive ETA increases that cumulatively exceed the threshold
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - ETA increases in small increments (each below threshold X individually) but cumulative increase exceeds X minutes
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip - 3- Simulate ETA increasing by small increments (e.g. 2 min then 2 min then 2 min) until cumulative increase exceeds X (e.g. X=5) - 4- Observe whether deviation is detected based on total increase or only individual incremental increases
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Active trip is visible - 3- ETA increases incrementally - 4- System evaluates the total ETA increase relative to the original planned ETA - when cumulative increase exceeds X minutes deviation is flagged - incremental sub-threshold increases alone do not suppress detection

Title: Verify deviation detection works correctly across all trip stops in a multi-stop trip
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active multi-stop trip is in progress - ETA deviation threshold is configured - Driver is progressing through multiple stops
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active multi-stop trip - 3- Simulate an ETA increase greater than X minutes for Stop 3 while Stops 1 and 2 have already been completed - 4- Observe whether deviation is detected for Stop 3
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Multi-stop trip is displayed - 3- Completed stops show no active deviation monitoring - 4- ETA increase for Stop 3 (the current next stop) exceeds threshold and deviation is correctly detected and flagged

Title: Verify that deviation detection is not affected by ETA fluctuations caused by GPS signal loss or inaccurate positioning
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip is in progress - GPS signal is temporarily lost or provides inaccurate position data - This causes a temporary spike in calculated ETA
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active trip - 3- Simulate GPS signal loss or inaccurate position causing ETA to spike artificially - 4- Observe whether a false deviation alert is triggered
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Active trip is visible - 3- GPS signal issue causes temporary ETA spike - 4- System either applies a smoothing or validation mechanism to avoid false deviation alerts - or clearly distinguishes GPS-caused ETA spikes from genuine deviation - no spurious alert is raised based on unreliable GPS data

Title: Verify that the system configurable deviation threshold accepts only valid positive integer or decimal values
Preconditions: User is logged in as System Administrator - System configuration panel is accessible - ETA deviation threshold input field is available
Steps(Step): 1- Log in as System Administrator - 2- Navigate to the system configuration panel - 3- Attempt to set the threshold to zero (0) - 4- Attempt to set the threshold to a negative number (-5) - 5- Attempt to set the threshold to a non-numeric value (e.g. "abc") - 6- Attempt to set the threshold to a valid value (e.g. 5) - 7- Save each configuration and observe results
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Admin portal loads - 2- Configuration panel is displayed - 3- Zero value is rejected or shows a validation error - 4- Negative number is rejected with validation error - 5- Non-numeric value is rejected with validation error - 6- Valid positive value is accepted and saved successfully - 7- Only valid configurations are persisted

Title: Verify deviation detection is scoped per trip segment and not across the entire trip ETA
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip with multiple segments is in progress - ETA for the overall trip is unchanged but ETA for the next stop in the current segment has increased
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view for an active segmented trip - 3- Ensure overall trip ETA has not changed significantly - 4- Simulate an ETA increase of more than X minutes for the immediately next stop in the current segment - 5- Observe whether deviation is detected
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Segmented trip is displayed - 3- Overall trip ETA is unchanged - 4- ETA for next stop in current segment increases beyond threshold - 5- System detects deviation based on the next stop ETA for the current segment - not based on overall trip ETA

Title: Verify that deviation detection is operational immediately when a new trip segment begins after the previous stop is completed
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - Driver has just completed a stop and a new segment to the next stop has begun - ETA deviation threshold is configured
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view - 3- Mark a stop as complete so driver begins traveling to the next stop - 4- Immediately simulate an ETA increase of more than X minutes for the newly active next stop - 5- Observe whether deviation is detected
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Stop is marked complete - 3- New segment begins with updated next stop target - 4- ETA increase for the new next stop exceeds threshold - 5- Deviation is detected immediately upon the new segment starting without a delay or reset period

Title: Verify that deviation detection correctly handles concurrent deviations on multiple active trips
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - Multiple active trips are in progress simultaneously - ETA deviations occur on more than one trip at the same time
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view showing multiple active trips - 3- Simulate ETA increases exceeding threshold X for two or more trips simultaneously - 4- Observe whether deviations are independently detected and flagged for each trip
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Multiple active trips are visible - 3- ETA increases occur simultaneously on multiple trips - 4- System independently detects and flags deviations for each affected trip without mixing data or missing any flagged trip

Title: Verify that break segments are correctly identified and excluded from deviation detection across different break types (scheduled and unscheduled)
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip includes both a scheduled break and a driver-initiated unscheduled break - ETA increases occur during both break travel segments
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view - 3- Observe driver traveling to a scheduled break location - simulate ETA increase beyond threshold - 4- Observe driver during an unscheduled break - simulate ETA increase beyond threshold - 5- Check whether deviation alerts are triggered for either break type
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Active trip with breaks is displayed - 3- ETA increase during scheduled break travel does NOT trigger deviation alert - 4- ETA increase during unscheduled break travel does NOT trigger deviation alert (if unscheduled breaks are treated as break travel) - 5- Neither break type causes a deviation flag

Title: Verify that deviation detection resumes correctly after end-of-shift pull-in is completed if additional trips are assigned
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - A driver has completed pull-in for an earlier shift and has been assigned a new trip - ETA deviation threshold is configured
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view - 3- Confirm driver has completed pull-in and a new trip has been assigned - 4- Simulate an ETA increase of more than X minutes for the first stop of the new trip - 5- Observe whether deviation is detected
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Driver's new trip assignment is visible - 3- New trip is confirmed as active - 4- ETA increase for first stop of new trip exceeds threshold - 5- Deviation is correctly detected for the new trip - pull-in exclusion from the previous shift does not suppress detection for the new trip

Title: Verify UI displays a clear and distinguishable deviation indicator when ETA threshold is exceeded for the next stop
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher - An active trip has triggered a deviation alert (ETA exceeded threshold) - Dispatcher is viewing the live map or trip list panel
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map view or trip list - 3- Identify a trip where ETA for the next stop has exceeded the deviation threshold - 4- Observe the visual indicator displayed for the deviated trip - 5- Verify the indicator is distinguishable from non-deviated trips
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map or trip list is displayed - 3- Deviated trip is identifiable - 4- A clear visual indicator (e.g. color change - icon - badge or warning label) is shown for the deviated trip - 5- Non-deviated trips do not show the deviation indicator - the differentiation is clear and unambiguous
